mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold, or the signal loses predictive power during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales and housing starts diverge, or quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are negative in >55% of months when industrial production increases, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of months when CPI exceeds the threshold, or the inflation signal fails to predict returns during periods of supply-driven price increases.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >50% of months following payroll increases, or the payroll signal shows no statistical correlation with equity returns over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak real GDP growth combined with stagnant retail sales indicates collapsing aggregate demand, forcing corporate earnings revisions downward and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal predicts positive returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses explanatory power when supply shocks dominate demand weakness.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and reduced factory utilization, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns are positive in >60% of months following an industrial production decline, or the signal fails to predict short-term equity movements during periods of inventory restocking.
